hard.next_step: July 2 Rick: Champion (Marcela in Mexico) is connecting internally with Procurement Selection Committee. Timing of next steps will be provided. Meanwhile we will invite to Horizon

north_star_verdict.headline: Brand-new Mexico S2C deal (created 2 Jul), champion Marcela opening the door to Del Monte's Procurement Selection Committee; 389 days to close, early Qualified stage
north_star_verdict.math: Created and qualified same day (2026-07-02); today 2026-07-06, so the deal is 4 days old. Close date 2027-07-30 is about 389 days out, squarely inside the typical 12-15 month enterprise S2C cycle. Time in Qualified stage: 4 days. No forward slip is possible on a 4-day-old record, and no deliverable is overdue. At Qualified the realistic next milestone is discovery depth, mapping the committee, and identifying the economic buyer/decision maker. Pace is appropriate for early-stage enterprise motion; the risk is depth of engagement, not timing.
evidence: Created and qualified 2026-07-02; stage moved Initial Interest to Qualified by Alexa Bradley that day (field history)

Ownership reassigned to Rick Taranek and Division set to New Markets on 2026-07-02 (field history)
About 389 days to close (2027-07-30), within the normal enterprise cycle band

Full S2C suite plus AI in scope: iContract, iSave, iSupplier, Merlin, Agentic AI, ANA

metrics.narrative: No business problem or quantified impact has been stated by Del Monte Mexico. There is no buyer call on record and no pain/value field is populated. The deal is 4 days old and in early Qualified stage, so the absence of quantified metrics is normal for the discovery phase rather than a red flag. The next move is a discovery call with Marcela and the Procurement Selection Committee to elicit the operational baseline (contracting cycle time, savings leakage, supplier onboarding effort) so a value case can be built.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named. There are zero OpportunityContactRole records and the Next Step (2026-07-02) references a 'Procurement Selection Committee' without naming the budget authority. In a Del Monte Mexico S2C motion the economic buyer is typically a senior procurement or finance leader who owns the committee's mandate, but the evidence does not yet identify that person. Champion Marcela is the internal connector; her own authority over budget is unconfirmed. The next move is to map the committee and identify who controls commercial terms and final sign-off.
sources: Next Step 2026-07-02 names the committee but no budget owner

decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria are documented and no scoring rubric or requirement list has been shared. There is no buyer call and no decision-criteria field populated. The product scope (iContract, iSave, iSupplier plus Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA) implies a full S2C evaluation likely to weigh AI/agentic capability, but the buyer has not stated what they will measure vendors against. The next move is a discovery session to surface their current stack, priorities, and the committee's must-haves.

decision_process.narrative: The Next Step (2026-07-02) signals a committee-driven selection: champion Marcela is 'connecting internally with the Procurement Selection Committee', which is typical for an enterprise S2C purchase. That tells us a structured, multi-person process is forming, but the committee membership, the formal stages (RFI/RFP/shortlist), the approval chain, and the timeline are all unknown; Marcela herself said timing of next steps will be provided. The next move is to ask Marcela for the committee roster, the evaluation timeline, and the approval chain in writing.
sources: Next Step 2026-07-02: Marcela connecting with Procurement Selection Committee

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, or partner detail exists yet. No contract-expiration or MSA/SOW field is populated and there is no buyer call. As a NEW-BUSINESS full-suite deal this will eventually run the standard relay (MSA, Order Form, SOW signed separately, InfoSec/DPA, and a Mexico-region PO that gates invoicing), and iSupplier in scope implies an ERP integration scoping step, but none of that is visible at 4 days old. The next move is to ask during discovery whether an incumbent contract is expiring (a potential forcing function) and what Del Monte Mexico's contracting timeline looks like.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. No pain field is populated and there is no call transcript. The full S2C plus AI scope implies manual contracting, limited supplier collaboration, and no AI-assisted negotiation as likely pain themes, but that is inferred from the solution set, not stated by Del Monte Mexico. The pain owner is presumably within the Procurement Selection Committee that Marcela is engaging. The next move is a discovery call to have the buyer name what is broken today and the cost of inaction.
sources: Product scope inferred: full S2C plus Merlin/Agentic AI/ANA

champion.narrative: Champion identified: Marcela (Mexico), named in the Next Step dated 2026-07-02 as the person connecting internally with the Procurement Selection Committee, which shows initial internal access and willingness to facilitate. What has developed is a live internal connector; what is still missing is her Salesforce contact role, her title, her authority within procurement, her relationship to the economic buyer, and any evidence of active advocacy (she has not appeared on a call and has committed only to provide timing). Her strength is 'developing' for exactly those reasons. The next move is to qualify Marcela's role and influence and secure her as an active advocate inside the committee.
sources: Next Step 2026-07-02: 'Champion (Marcela in Mexico) is connecting internally with Procurement Selection Committee'

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source: Competitors__c is empty, no free-text competition field is populated, and there is no buyer call to reveal the current stack or shortlist. It is unknown whether this is a sole-source engagement, an undisclosed RFP shortlist, or a replacement of an incumbent, so no competitive wedge can be asserted honestly. For a 4-day-old deal this is expected rather than a settled sole-source. The next move is to ask during discovery what Del Monte Mexico uses today for contracting, savings, and supplier management and who else is being evaluated.

deal_movement.summary: The only movement is the deal's creation and same-day qualification on 2026-07-02: stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified, ownership moved to Rick Taranek, and Division was set to New Markets. The single live pulse is the Next Step of that date, where champion Marcela is engaging the Procurement Selection Committee and will provide timing. No buyer-facing deliverable has occurred and no inbound has been logged since.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a fresh, single-threaded Qualified deal: convert the sole champion relationship into a mapped, multi-threaded committee read, identify the budget owner, and establish the competitive landscape before positioning.
flags: Single-threaded on Marcela: multi-thread into the Procurement Selection Committee before it forms its shortlist without us.
Economic buyer and decision maker are unmapped: identify who owns the S2C budget inside the committee early, while access is easy.
No competitive intelligence: establish Del Monte Mexico's current contracting/savings stack and any RFP shortlist during the first discovery call.
